title: Post-Bid Follow-Up After Pricing Submitted
openingLine: Yeah I got your numbers. Bid was last Tuesday. Haven't heard anything yet. We'll let you know if we win it.
customerContext: You're a busy estimator who has 12 active bids in flight. You submitted on this one and you've moved on mentally. You don't have specific feedback for Wenger — you just don't know yet whether you won or lost. You're not unfriendly but you're not going to volunteer information either.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Bid opened Tuesday. We were second place — about 2.5% behind the low bidder.
Low bidder used a competitor cabinet that was about $40K cheaper than your number.
Northway Constructors. They're a regional player. Aggressive on price.
Owner has 30 days to make award. Could still ask for VE before signing.
Both bids came in over budget — but Northway only by $50K, we were $90K over.
If we offered a VE alternative, the owner might rebid. They've done that before.
We have two more education projects coming up. I'll keep you in the loop on those.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked if there were any questions on the quote
Researched bid results (school board docs, owner contacts, Google)

Solution Framing.behavior: Offered to help win the project if it came in over budget

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Continued outreach when initial response was thin

BANT.behavior: Discussed over-budget considerations and VE pathways

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Followed up within 1-2 days of bid opening
Set tasks 1-2 weeks until contact established
Set tasks 2-3 weeks out after initial contact
Set additional tasks based on findings

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Established contact and a clear next step
